Metadata.title: Quiet Riots
Metadata.description: Organiza-te em torno de questões partilhadas. Encontra a tua comunidade. Age em conjunto.

Footer.mission: Mudar mais para melhor nas nossas vidas do que ousamos imaginar ser possível.
Footer.powered: Quiet Riots · Powered by OpenClaw AI Agent

Home.headline: A mudança não precisa de ser barulhenta para ser poderosa
Home.description: Encontra outros que partilham as tuas questões. Age em conjunto. O Quiet Riots liga pessoas em torno de frustrações comuns e transforma queixas em ação coletiva.
Home.findYourIssueDesc: Pesquisa qualquer frustração — comboios, internet, saúde, clima. Se existir, mostramos-te quantas outras pessoas a partilham.
Home.useThePivotDesc: Vê a mesma questão em todas as organizações, ou todas as questões numa organização. Como dançarinos de tango a mudar de direção.
Home.takeActionDesc: Ideias, ações e comunidade — personalizadas ao teu tempo e competências. Pequenas ações somam-se a grandes mudanças.
Home.missionQuote: "Mudar mais para melhor nas nossas vidas do que ousamos imaginar ser possível."
Home.basedOn: Baseado no livro de 2014 Quiet Riots de Simon Darling

CookieConsent.description: Usamos cookies para te manter autenticado e melhorar a tua experiência. Os cookies de análise ajudam-nos a perceber como o site é utilizado.
CookieConsent.essentialDescription: Necessários para o funcionamento do site. Não podem ser desativados.

RejectionReasons.title: Why Quiet Riots Get Rejected
RejectionReasons.subtitle: Understanding the review process
RejectionReasons.intro: Every suggestion for a new Quiet Riot is reviewed by a Setup Guide to ensure it fits the platform and can make a real difference. Here are the reasons a suggestion might not be approved:
RejectionReasons.closeToExistingTitle: Close to an Existing Quiet Riot
RejectionReasons.closeToExistingP1: If your suggestion is very similar to a Quiet Riot that already exists, we'll merge your suggestion into the existing one. This keeps the community focused and prevents fragmentation. The more people behind one Quiet Riot, the bigger the impact.
RejectionReasons.closeToExistingP2: When this happens, you'll be automatically added to the existing Quiet Riot so you can start taking action right away.
RejectionReasons.aboutPeopleP1: Quiet Riots are about shared issues, not about targeting individuals. We focus on systemic problems — things like poor broadband, rising energy costs, or inadequate healthcare — rather than personal grievances with specific people.
RejectionReasons.aboutPeopleP2: If you're frustrated with a specific person or public figure, try framing it as the underlying issue instead. For example, instead of targeting a CEO, suggest a Quiet Riot about the company's practices.
RejectionReasons.illegalP1: We can't host Quiet Riots that promote or organise illegal activities. This includes anything that violates applicable laws, promotes violence, or could put people at risk.
RejectionReasons.otherP1: Occasionally a suggestion might not fit for another reason — perhaps it's too vague, or the Setup Guide needs more information. In these cases, you'll receive a message explaining why and what you can do next.
RejectionReasons.whatNextIntro: If your suggestion is rejected, you can always:
RejectionReasons.reframe: Reframe and try again — adjust the name or description to better fit the guidelines
RejectionReasons.joinExisting: Join an existing Quiet Riot — your issue might already be covered
RejectionReasons.replyGuide: Reply to the Setup Guide — if you think the rejection was a mistake, send a message explaining your case

AnonymousInfo.title: Anonymous First Rioters
AnonymousInfo.subtitle: What being anonymous means
AnonymousInfo.intro: When someone starts a new Quiet Riot, they become the First Quiet Rioter. This is a badge of honour — the person who had the courage to speak up first.
AnonymousInfo.choosingIntro: Not everyone wants their name attached to an issue publicly. That's completely fine. When the First Quiet Rioter chooses to stay anonymous:
AnonymousInfo.choosingHidden: Their name and profile picture are hidden from the issue page
AnonymousInfo.choosingBadge: An anonymous badge is shown instead, so people know someone started it without knowing who
AnonymousInfo.choosingGuides: The Setup Guides still know who started it (for moderation purposes)
AnonymousInfo.choosingChange: The First Rioter can change their mind later and choose to be publicly recognised
AnonymousInfo.whyIntro: There are many valid reasons to stay anonymous:
AnonymousInfo.whySensitive: The issue is sensitive or controversial
AnonymousInfo.whyWork: You work for a company related to the issue
AnonymousInfo.whyCause: You prefer to let the cause speak for itself
AnonymousInfo.changingP1: If you started a Quiet Riot anonymously but later decide you want to be recognised, you can update your preference at any time through your inbox or by messaging a Setup Guide.

SuggestPage.title: Suggest a New Quiet Riot
SuggestPage.subtitle: Can't find your issue? Suggest a new Quiet Riot and we'll review it.
